metadata.title: Subtractive Mixture Models via Squaring:Representation and Learning

sections.title: 0 Mixture models
content: p(X) = Σ w_i p_i(X) subject tow_i ≥ 0

2 How much more expressive are they?
3 What is their relationship with other models?

sections.title: 1 How to learn subtractive mixture models?
content: p(X) = Σ w_i p_i(X) wherew_i ∈ ℝ
How to ensure p(X) is non-negative? Impose ad-hoc constraints or use squaring.

figures.description: Deep subtractive mixture circuit architecture showing squaring layers

sections.title: 1 Squaring mixtures ...
content: Tractable marginalization is supported by exponential families and splines components.

figures.description: Renormalization function plot for squared mixtures

sections.title: 2 Build deep mixtures with layers as "Lego blocks"

figures.description: Lego blocks representation of deep mixture layers

sections.title: Theorem. exponential separation
content: There is a class of distributions F over variables X that can be compactly represented as a shallow squared mixture with negative weights, but the smallest structured decomposable additive-only mixture of any depth computing any F has size 2^Ω(|X|).

figures.description: Comparison of deep additive-only mixtures vs squared subtractive mixture model

sections.title: 3 Understanding the expressiveness of other models
content: Density estimation results across various datasets (Power, Gas, Hepmass, MiniBooNE, BSDS300).

Density estimation performance comparison bottom row

sections.title: References
content: [1] B. Zhang and C. Zhang. "Finite mixture models with negative components". In: MLDM. Springer, 2005.[2] G. Rabusseau and F. Denis. "Learning negative mixture models by tensor decompositions". In: arXiv preprint, 2014.[3] R. Jiang, M. J. Zuo, and H. Li. "Weibull and inverse Weibull mixture models allowing negative weights". In: Reliability Engineering, 2019.[4] J. Martens and V. Medabalimi. "On the expressive efficiency of sum product networks". In: arXiv preprint, 2014.[5] A. de Colnet and S. Mengel. "A Compilation of Succinctness Results for Arithmetic Circuits". In: KR, 2021.[6] I. Glasser et al. "Expressive power of tensor-network factorizations for probabilistic modeling". In: NeurIPS, 2019.[7] A. Rudi and C. Ciliberto. "PSD Representations for Effective Probability Models". In: NeurIPS, 2021.[8] H. Zhang et al. "Tractable Control for Autoregressive Language Generation". In: ICML, 2023.
